expectations.text: Recommends placing both brand deals in the back half (after minute 6)
expectations.evidence: Option 1 suggests placing the first deal at 'minutes 2–3' — well before minute 6. While Option 2 (both in the back half) is also presented, the response offers Option 1 as a valid equal alternative. A response that recommends a front-loaded option as viable does not pass this expectation.

expectations.text: Does not recommend placing any brand deal in the first 5 minutes
expectations.evidence: Option 1 explicitly recommends 'First brand deal around minutes 2–3' — directly within the first 5 minutes and presented as a legitimate option.

expectations.text: Suggests integration strategies tied to the obstacle course competition context
expectations.evidence: Response suggests energy drinks/sports nutrition shown during the competition, athletic apparel as prizes, mobile games as side-challenges, and tech/wearables to track performance — all contextually tied to the obstacle course format.

expectations.text: Lists specific common mistakes to avoid
expectations.evidence: Response has a 'Common Mistakes to Avoid' section listing 5 items including back-to-back ads, unrelated sponsors, scripted reads, excessive length, and pre-competition placement.

expectations.text: Notes that 2 brand deals in a 12-minute video is manageable if placed correctly
expectations.evidence: The response does not explicitly address whether 2 deals in 12 minutes is manageable. It jumps straight into placement options without framing the feasibility question.

expectations.text: Advises separating the two brand deals (not back-to-back)
expectations.evidence: Mistake #1 in the Common Mistakes section states: 'Doing both ads back-to-back — that creates a long dead zone in the middle of the video.'

claims.claim: Option 2 (both in back half) is probably safer
claims.evidence: The response itself acknowledges Option 2 is safer, but then gives equal weight to Option 1 which violates the placement principle.

claims.claim: Each segment should be 30–60 seconds max
claims.evidence: This matches the skill's guidance and is correctly stated in the General Tips section.

eval_feedback.overall: No additional suggestions. The placement expectations correctly identified the baseline's core failure — presenting a front-loaded option as valid.
